subaward_description:
DRS. WANG AND KIMMEL STARTED WORKING TOGETHER IN EARLY 2020 ON THE DEVELOPMENT OF A
COMPUTATIONALLY EFFICIENT CANCER SUBCLONAL RECONSTRUCTION METHOD, CLIP (CLONAL STRUCTURE
IDENTIFICATION THROUGH PENALIZING PAIRWISE DIFFERENCE). IT UTILIZES A REGULARIZED MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD
FRAMEWORK AND CAN BE APPLIED TO BOTH CHROMOSOMAL REGIONS WITH AND WITHOUT COPY NUMBER
ALTERATIONS (CNAS). WHILE MANY OTHER METHODS RELY ON CNAS AND CANNOT WORK WITHOUT CNA
INFORMATION. CLIP IS RECOGNIZED AS A PART OF THE PCAWG STUDY, AN INTERNATIONAL COLLABORATION TO
IDENTIFY COMMON PATTERS OF MUTATIONS IN OVER TWO THOUSAND AND SIX HUNDRED CANCER WHOLE
GENOMES FROM THE INTERNATIONAL CANCER GENOME CONSORTIUM.
ON THE OTHER HAND, DR. KIMMEL AND HIS CO-WORKERS, DEVELOPED A MATHEMATICAL FRAMEWORK FOR
ESTIMATION OF THE TIMING OF SELECTIVE SWEEPS IN TUMOR HISTORY (DINH ET AL., STATISTICAL SCIENCE,
2020). THIS METHODOLOGY REQUIRES INFORMATION ON CLUSTERING OF GENOMIC DATA WITH RESPECT TO COPY
NUMBER VARIATIONS, POINT MUTATIONS AND PLOIDY, AND PURITY, WHICH CLIP CAN PROVIDE. IT ALSO NEEDS
DEVELOPMENT OF NUMERICAL PROCEDURES TO FIT THE MODELS TO MORE COMPLICATED EVOLUTIONARY
SCENARIOS. DR KIMMEL WILL INTEGRATE CLIP WITH EVOLUTIONARY MODELS IN AIM 1 AND PROVIDE INFERENCE
OF NEUTRAL DRIFT VERSUS SELECTION SWEEPS IN TUMOR EVOLUTION IN THE PROPOSED DATASETS IN AIM 2

subaward_description:
"DISTRIBUTED ACOUSTIC SENSING (DAS) IS A RAPIDLY EVOLVING TECHNOLOGY THAT USES FIBER-OPTIC CABLES AS ARRAYS OF SENSORS CAPABLE
OF MEASURING SPATIALLY RESOLVED DYNAMIC STRAIN. DAS HAS THE POTENTIAL TO REVOLUTIONIZE SEISMIC MONITORING BY DRAMATICALLY
INCREASING MEASUREMENT DENSITY OVER LARGE DISTANCES THAT CAN REACH TENS OF KILOMETERS, AND BY MAKING OBSERVATIONS IN
DIVERSE ENVIRONMENTS SUCH AS IN CITIES OR ON THE OCEAN FLOOR, IN WHICH TRADITIONAL SEISMIC MONITORING WOULD BE EITHER
DIFFICULT OR IMPOSSIBLE. DAS DIFFERS FROM CONVENTIONAL SEISMIC MONITORING BECAUSE THE FUNDAMENTAL MEASUREMENT BEARS A
MORE COMPLEX RELATIONSHIP TO THE CAUSATIVE WAVEFIELD. FOR THAT REASON, IT IS ESSENTIAL TO UNDERSTAND HOW IT IS SIMILAR TO,
AND HOW IT DIFFERS FROM, CONVENTIONAL SEISMIC MEASUREMENTS. THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A QUANTITATIVE
UNDERSTANDING OF THE POTENTIAL OF DAS FOR SEISMIC MONITORING USING DATA COLLECTED IN A WIDE RANGE OF ENVIRONMENTS.
THE PROPOSED PROGRAM OF RESEARCH WILL SUPPORT A BROAD EFFORT OF LEADING DAS RESEARCHERS AT MULTIPLE INSTITUTIONS WITH THE
AIM OF CARRYING OUT RESEARCH THAT WILL LEAD TO A COMPREHENSIVE UNDERSTANDING OF THE STRENGTHS AND LIMITATIONS OF THIS NEW
TECHNOLOGY SUCH THAT ITS POTENTIAL FOR SEISMIC MONITORING CAN BE FULLY REALIZED. IN YEAR 1, THE PROJECT TEAM WILL BE ORGANIZED,
VALUABLE EXISTING DATASETS WILL BE ASSEMBLED, AND PRELIMINARY ANALYSES WILL BE UNDERTAKEN. THEN, WE WILL PURSUE TARGETED
PROJECTS IN YEARS 2 THROUGH 5 OF THE PROJECT THAT ARE EXPECTED TO DEMONSTRATE THE VALUE OF DAS FOR FULL-SCALE DEPLOYMENTS
AND EXPLORE POSSIBLE EXTENSIONS TO THE CURRENT STATE-OF-THE-ART, PARTICULARLY IN THE CONTEXT OF EXPLOSION DETECTION AND
MONITORING."

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
DR. REBECCA RICHARDS-KORTUM IS CO-PRINCIPLE INVESTIGATOR ON THE GRANT AND WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE TECHNICAL AND ENGINEERING ASPECTS OF THE PROJECT INCLUDING MANAGEMENT AND MAINTENANCE OF THE IMAGING DEVICES. IMAGE ANALYSIS OF THE OBTAINED PATIENT IMAGES WILL BE EVALUATED BY RIEC ENGINEERS FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF IMAGE ANALYSIS ALGORITHMS.

STATEMENT OF WORK:
REBECCA RICHARDS-KORTUM LABORATORY
DEPT. OF BIOENGINEERING
RICE UNIVERSITY

DESPITE ADVANCES IN CHEMORADIATION THERAPY, THE 5-YEAR SURVIVAL RATE FROM ESOPHAGEAL SQUAMOUS CELL CANCER (SCC) REMAINS A DISMAL 15% DUE TO DIAGNOSIS AT A LATE, INCURABLE STAGE. IN HIGH-RISK PATIENTS SCREENING AND SURVEILLANCE PROTOCOLS HAVE BEEN IMPLEMENTED WITH LIMITED SUCCESS. IN NORTHERN CHINA, WHERE THERE IS A VERY HIGH PREVALENCE OF SCC (1/1000), THE MOST WIDELY USED METHOD OF ENDOSCOPIC EVALUATION INVOLVES LUGOLÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S IODINE STAINING OF MUCOSA WITH TARGETED BIOPSY OF ABNORMAL (UNSTAINED) AREAS. WHILE LUGOLÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S SIGNIFICANTLY INCREASES THE SENSITIVITY OF STANDARD WHITE-LIGHT ENDOSCOPY, SPECIFICITY REMAINS POOR AS INFLAMMATION AND OTHER BENIGN MUCOSAL CHANGES MAY MIMIC NEOPLASIA. HIGH-RESOLUTION IMAGING WITH CONFOCAL ENDOMICROSCOPY HAS BEEN SHOWN TO DRAMATICALLY ENHANCE THE DIAGNOSTIC ACCURACY AND YIELD OF LUGOLÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S CHROMOENDOSCOPY AND OTHER WIDEFIELD TECHNOLOGIES IN LARGE REFERRAL CENTERS IN INDUSTRIALIZED COUNTRIES.  HOWEVER, THESE EXISTING PLATFORMS ARE COSTLY (> $150,000) AND ONLY AVAILABLE IN A HANDFUL OF TERTIARY CENTERS WORLDWIDE.
DR. REBECCA RICHARDS-KORTUMÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S LABORATORY AT RICE UNIVERSITY IS DEVELOPING LOW-COST, HIGH-RESOLUTION FIBER-OPTIC IMAGING SYSTEMS WHICH CAN BE COMBINED WITH CURRENT WHITE-LIGHT ENDOSCOPY.  WITHIN THIS RESEARCH PROPOSAL, WE WILL DESIGN, ASSEMBLE, AND TEST A HIGH-RESOLUTION MICROENDOSCOPE (HRME) SYSTEM FOR USE ALONGSIDE THE STANDARD ENDOSCOPE.  
WE WILL ALSO DEVELOP THE NECESSARY SOFTWARE NEEDED TO CONTROL THE HRME AND COLLECT IMAGES.  WE WILL TRAIN THE CLINICAL STAFF AT THE FIRST UNIVERSITY HOSPITAL, JILIN, CHINA IN THE USE OF THE HRME, AND IN INTERPRETATION OF THE IMAGES ACQUIRED WITH THE DEVICE.  WE WILL DEVELOP IMAGE ANALYSIS ALGORITHMS TO EXTRACT QUANTITATIVE PARAMETERS FROM TISSUE IMAGES AND ESTABLISH THE DEGREE OF CORRELATION BETWEEN THESE VALUES AND THE DIAGNOSIS PROVIDED BY HISTOPATHOLOGY.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: Microbiota-targeted approaches to resolve dysbiosis-induced AD neuropathology following brain injury. - PROJECT SUMMARY / ABSTRACT Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease that results in cognitive decline. The neuroinflammatory events that are associated with the pathology of AD exacerbate neurodegeneration. Elderly people with dementia or AD have a higher risk of falling because their cognitive function is affected. They lose their orientation and are very prone to breaking their hips, other bones, or even more severe falls that can cause brain trauma. Although they are treated in the hospitals with subsequent rehabilitation, their weakened immune state typically affects their recovery phases. We need to develop new treatments for AD patients who have had a traumatic brain injury (TBI). This vulnerable population could be particularly amenable to precision-microbiota therapy. Although not fully understood, the brain-gut-microbiota axis plays a major role in the onset and severity of many neurological diseases. We plan to develop precision-microbiota therapy to protect against neurodegeneration and functional behavior in AD mice after TBI. The gut microbiome is emerging as an essential neuromodulator of brain-gut axis signaling. It can significantly impact brain inflammation and outcome after CNS trauma and alter anxiety- and depression-like behaviors. This research proposal is based on the scientific premise that gut bacteria can impact behavior and that brain injury can disrupt diversity in a healthy gut microbiome. The overarching hypothesis is that the gut microbiota dysfunction caused by brain trauma contributes directly to neuroinflammation and neurodegeneration responses in AD mice, and the microbiota modulation will delay the progression of AD neuropathology. Our preliminary data indicate how the microbiota of AD mice affected adversely to TBI outcomes and how probiotics help brain recovery in C57B6/J young mice. This proposal investigates the mechanistic linkage between gut microbiota and AD progression following TBI and explores potential intervention strategies. We will test this premise with the following three Specific Aims: we 1) will determine if the gut microbiome plays a role in accelerating AD pathology and cognitive decline induced by TBI, and 2) will investigate the impact of fecal transplants on AD pathology and cognitive decline after TBI, and 3) will assess the ability of multi-strain probiotics to reduce AD pathology after TBI. We expect to identify a particularly vulnerable patient population by defining which inflammatory responses associated with brain trauma accelerate AD pathogenesis and are regulated by brain-gut-microbiome signals. Our approach is significant because we will establish a “translatable” foundation for the potential of therapeutic approaches for AD after TBI by using the gut as a non-CNS target of precision-microbiota therapy.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: MUSCLE LOSS DUE TO REDUCED LOADING IN MICROGRAVITY REMAINS MAJOR LIMITING ISSUE FOR LONG-TERM SPACEFLIGHT. THOUGH EXERCISE COUNTERMEASURES HAVE BEEN DEVELOPED AND IMPLEMENTED ON THE ISS  QUANTITATIVE METHODS FOR PREDICTING HOW SKELETAL MUSCLE ADAPTS STRUCTURALLY AND FUNCTIONALLY OVER TIME IN RESPONSE TO EXERCISE IN A MICROGRAVITY ENVIRONMENT DO NOT YET EXIST. A COMPUTATIONAL MODELING APPROACH THAT CAN PREDICT CHANGES IN MUSCLE PROPERTIES AS A FUNCTION OF TIME IN MICROGRAVITY AND A PREDETERMINED AMOUNT OF IN-FLIGHT EXERCISE STIMULUS WILL FACILITATE THE DESIGN OF EXERCISE PROGRAMS THAT CAN BE EMPLOYED BOTH PRE-FLIGHT AND IN-FLIGHT TO EFFECTIVELY COMBAT MUSCLE ATROPHY AND ENHANCE MUSCLE STRENGTH BOTH DURING AND AFTER FLIGHT. THIS PROPOSAL SEEKS TO DEVELOP A MULTISCALE COMPUTATIONAL MODEL THAT CAN PREDICT CHANGES IN MUSCLE STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION IN RESPONSE TO BOTH MICROGRAVITY AND IN-FLIGHT EXERCISE. OUR MODELING APPROACH WILL UNITE -- INTO A COHESIVE  USER-FRIENDLY  AND OPEN-SOURCE FRAMEWORK -- COMPUTATIONAL SIMULATIONS AT DIFFERENT LEVELS OF SPATIAL SCALE  INCLUDING: DYNAMIC OPENSIM SIMULATIONS AT THE WHOLE BODY LEVEL  FINITE-ELEMENT SIMULATIONS OF MUSCLE TISSUE  AND AGENT-BASED SIMULATIONS OF MUSCLE CELLS THAT INCLUDE THE BIOCHEMICAL AND BIOMECHANICAL PROCESSES THAT DRIVE INDIVIDUAL MUSCLE CELL ADAPTATIONS. THIS LEVEL OF INTEGRATION IS NECESSARY BECAUSE  THOUGH THEY ARE OFTEN ONLY CONSIDERED IN ISOLATION  FACTORS AT ALL OF THESE SCALES INFLUENCE THE DEGREE OF MUSCLE ATROPHY DURING PROLONGED SPACEFLIGHT. OUR TEAM BRINGS TOGETHER HIGHLY UNIQUE EXPERTISE: SILVIA BLEMKER (MODELING OF MUSCLE MECHANICS/PHYSIOLOGY)  B.J. FREGLY (DYNAMIC MODELING OF MOVEMENT)  AND SHAYN PEIRCE-COTTLER (AGENT- BASED MODELING AND CELLULAR/TISSUE ADAPTATION). OUR PROPOSED MODEL WILL INTEGRATE WITH OPENSIM BIOMECHANICS SIMULATION SOFTWARE AND WILL BE QUANTITATIVELY VERIFIED AND VALIDATED FOLLOWING THE STANDARD OF NASA-STD-7009. DOCUMENTATION THAT DESCRIBES THE MODELING APPROACHES USED  AS WELL AS A MODEL OPERATIONS GUIDE  WILL BE PROVIDED AS DELIVERABLES. THE SOFTWARE SOURCE CODE WILL ALSO BE PROVIDED TO NASA AS AN OPEN-SOURCE PRODUCT.
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
STATEMENT OF WORK
RICE UNIVERSITY
FOR THE PROPOSED PROJECT, RESEARCHERS AT RICE UNIVERSITY WILL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING TASKS:
1) IDENTIFICATION OF GROUND-BASED AND SPACE-BASED BIOMECHANICAL EXERCISE DATA TO BE
USED FOR OPENSIM MODEL VALIDATION (GROUND-BASED DATA) AND PREDICTIONS (SPACE-BASED
DATA) OF LOWER EXTREMITY NET MUSCLE MOMENTS. SPECIFIC DATA TO BE SOUGHT INCLUDE BODY
MOTION, EXERCISE DEVICE MOTION, AND FOOT-DEVICE REACTION FORCES. POTENTIAL SOURCES OF
DATA INCLUDE YOUTUBE AND NASA.
2) DEVELOPMENT OF A NOMINAL ASTRONAUT MUSCULOSKELETAL MODEL IN OPENSIM. KEY
REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MODEL ARE SMOOTH AND WELL-DEFINED MUSCLE-TENDON LENGTHS,
VELOCITIES, AND MOMENT ARMS FOR THE RANGES AND COMBINATIONS OF JOINT MOTIONS TO BE
SIMULATED FOR THIS PROJECT.
3) DEVELOPMENT OF EXERCISE DEVICE MODELS FOR THE ARED, T2 TREADMILL, AND CEVIS
ERGOMETER IN OPENSIM. FOR THE ARED, A MODEL ALREADY EXISTS FOR SQUAT EXERCISE, AND
THIS MODEL WILL BE MODIFIED TO ACCOMMODATE DEAD LIFT AND HEEL RAISE EXERCISES. FOR THE
T2 TREADMILL AND CEVIS ERGOMETER, OPENSIM DEVICE MODELS WILL BE CREATED USING
COMPUTER AIDED DESIGN GEOMETRY DATA PROVIDED BY NASA.
4) CREATION OF A COMPUTATIONAL PIPELINE IN MATLAB THAT PERFORMS ALL STEPS NECESSARY TO
GENERATE ASTRONAUT-SPECIFIC NET MUSCLE MOMENT ESTIMATES FOR EACH OF THE FIVE EXERCISES
TO BE SIMULATED IN OPENSIM. THE PIPELINE WILL AUTOMATICALLY SCALE THE NOMINAL ASTRONAUT
MUSCULOSKELETAL MODEL IN OPENSIM, INCORPORATE THE SCALED MODEL INTO THE FIVE OPENSIM
EXERCISE DEVICE MODELS, AND RUN A FORWARD DYNAMIC SIMULATION USING EACH COMBINED
ASTRONAUT-DEVICE MODEL TO CALCULATE THE NET MUSCLE MOMENTS REQUIRED FOR THE ASTRONAUT
TO PRODUCE A SPECIFIED EXERCISE MOTION.
5) DEVELOPMENT OF AN AUTOMATED MUSCLE-TENDON MODEL CALIBRATION PROCEDURE IN MATLAB.
GIVEN AN ASTRONAUTÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S HEIGHT AND MASS AND SCALED OPENSIM MODEL, THE PROCEDURE WILL
AUTOMATICALLY SPECIFY THE FOLLOWING PROPERTIES FOR ALL LOWER EXTREMITY MUSCLES IN THE
MODEL: PEAK ISOMETRIC STRENGTH, TENDON SLACK LENGTH, OPTIMAL MUSCLE FIBER LENGTH,
PENNATION ANGLE, MAXIMUM SHORTENING VELOCITY FOR SLOW AND FAST TWITCH FIBERS, AND
PERCENT OF MUSCLE FIBERS THAT ARE SLOW AND FAST TWITCH.
6) DEVELOPMENT OF AUTOMATED MUSCLE FORCE OPTIMIZATION PROCEDURES IN MATLAB TO
ESTIMATE LOWER EXTREMITY MUSCLE FORCES FOR EACH OF THE FIVE EXERCISES SIMULATED IN
OPENSIM. THE PROCEDURES WILL TAKE NET MUSCLE MOMENTS ALONG WITH MUSCLE-TENDON
LENGTHS, VELOCITIES, AND MOMENT ARMS CALCULATED BY OPENSIM AS INPUTS AND WILL OUTPUT
MUSCLE FORCE ESTIMATES FOR DIFFERENT ASSUMPTION ABOUT MUSCLE FIBER TYPES, TENDON
COMPLIANCE, AND OPTIMIZATION COST FUNCTION.
7) CREATION OF AN APPLICATION PROGRAMMING INTERFACE SO THAT MUSCLE EXCITATIONS,
ACTIVATIONS, FIBER FORCES, FIBER LENGTHS, AND FIBER VELOCITIES CAN BE PASSED TO DOWNSTREAM
THREE-DIMENSIONAL MUSCLE MODELS.

OUR TEAM BRINGS TOGETHER HIGHLY UNIQUE EXPERTISE: SILVIA BLEMKER (MODELING OF MUSCLE MECHANICS/PHYSIOLOGY)  B.J. FREGLY (DYNAMIC MODELING OF MOVEMENT)  AND SHAYN PEIRCE-COTTLER (AGENT- BASED MODELING AND CELLULAR/TISSUE ADAPTATION). OUR PROPOSED MODEL WILL INTEGRATE WITH OPENSIM BIOMECHANICS SIMULATION SOFTWARE AND WILL BE QUANTITATIVELY VERIFIED AND VALIDATED FOLLOWING THE STANDARD OF NASA-STD-7009. DOCUMENTATION THAT DESCRIBES THE MODELING APPROACHES USED  AS WELL AS A MODEL OPERATIONS GUIDE  WILL BE PROVIDED AS DELIVERABLES. THE SOFTWARE SOURCE CODE WILL ALSO BE PROVIDED TO NASA AS AN OPEN-SOURCE PRODUCT.
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
STATEMENT OF WORK RICE UNIVERSITY
FOR THE PROPOSED PROJECT, RESEARCHERS AT RICE UNIVERSITY WILL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING TASKS:
1) IDENTIFICATION OF GROUND-BASED AND SPACE-BASED BIOMECHANICAL EXERCISE DATA TO BE USED FOR OPENSIM MODEL VALIDATION (GROUND-BASED DATA) AND PREDICTIONS (SPACE-BASED DATA) OF LOWER EXTREMITY NET MUSCLE MOMENTS. SPECIFIC DATA TO BE SOUGHT INCLUDE BODY MOTION, EXERCISE DEVICE MOTION, AND FOOT-DEVICE REACTION FORCES. POTENTIAL SOURCES OF DATA INCLUDE YOUTUBE AND NASA.
2) DEVELOPMENT OF A NOMINAL ASTRONAUT MUSCULOSKELETAL MODEL IN OPENSIM. KEY
REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MODEL ARE SMOOTH AND WELL-DEFINED MUSCLE-TENDON LENGTHS,
VELOCITIES, AND MOMENT ARMS FOR THE RANGES AND COMBINATIONS OF JOINT MOTIONS TO BE SIMULATED FOR THIS PROJECT.
3) DEVELOPMENT OF EXERCISE DEVICE MODELS FOR THE ARED, T2 TREADMILL, AND CEVIS
ERGOMETER IN OPENSIM. FOR THE ARED, A MODEL ALREADY EXISTS FOR SQUAT EXERCISE, AND THIS MODEL WILL BE MODIFIED TO ACCOMMODATE DEAD LIFT AND HEEL RAISE EXERCISES. FOR THE T2 TREADMILL AND CEVIS ERGOMETER, OPENSIM DEVICE MODELS WILL BE CREATED USING COMPUTER AIDED DESIGN GEOMETRY DATA PROVIDED BY NASA.
4) CREATION OF A COMPUTATIONAL PIPELINE IN MATLAB THAT PERFORMS ALL STEPS NECESSARY TO GENERATE ASTRONAUT-SPECIFIC NET MUSCLE MOMENT ESTIMATES FOR EACH OF THE FIVE EXERCISES TO BE SIMULATED IN OPENSIM. THE PIPELINE WILL AUTOMATICALLY SCALE THE NOMINAL ASTRONAUT MUSCULOSKELETAL MODEL IN OPENSIM, INCORPORATE THE SCALED MODEL INTO THE FIVE OPENSIM EXERCISE DEVICE MODELS, AND RUN A FORWARD DYNAMIC SIMULATION USING EACH COMBINED ASTRONAUT-DEVICE MODEL TO CALCULATE THE NET MUSCLE MOMENTS REQUIRED FOR THE ASTRONAUT TO PRODUCE A SPECIFIED EXERCISE MOTION.
5) DEVELOPMENT OF AN AUTOMATED MUSCLE-TENDON MODEL CALIBRATION PROCEDURE IN MATLAB. GIVEN AN ASTRONAUTÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S HEIGHT AND MASS AND SCALED OPENSIM MODEL, THE PROCEDURE WILL AUTOMATICALLY SPECIFY THE FOLLOWING PROPERTIES FOR ALL LOWER EXTREMITY MUSCLES IN THE MODEL: PEAK ISOMETRIC STRENGTH, TENDON SLACK LENGTH, OPTIMAL MUSCLE FIBER LENGTH, PENNATION ANGLE, MAXIMUM SHORTENING VELOCITY FOR SLOW AND FAST TWITCH FIBERS, AND PERCENT OF MUSCLE FIBERS THAT ARE SLOW AND FAST TWITCH.
6) DEVELOPMENT OF AUTOMATED MUSCLE FORCE OPTIMIZATION PROCEDURES IN MATLAB TO
ESTIMATE LOWER EXTREMITY MUSCLE FORCES FOR EACH OF THE FIVE EXERCISES SIMULATED IN OPENSIM. THE PROCEDURES WILL TAKE NET MUSCLE MOMENTS ALONG WITH MUSCLE-TENDON LENGTHS, VELOCITIES, AND MOMENT ARMS CALCULATED BY OPENSIM AS INPUTS AND WILL OUTPUT MUSCLE FORCE ESTIMATES FOR DIFFERENT ASSUMPTION ABOUT MUSCLE FIBER TYPES, TENDON COMPLIANCE, AND OPTIMIZATION COST FUNCTION.
7) CREATION OF AN APPLICATION PROGRAMMING INTERFACE SO THAT MUSCLE EXCITATIONS,
ACTIVATIONS, FIBER FORCES, FIBER LENGTHS, AND FIBER VELOCITIES CAN BE PASSED TO DOWNSTREAM THREE-DIMENSIONAL MUSCLE MODELS.

AMEND1   $24,064

DR. KIMMEL AND HIS GRADUATE STUDENT SIYI CHEN WILL BE PARTICIPATING IN THE BRANCHED PROCESS MODELING AND STATISTICAL ANALYSIS OF DATA (AIM 2). SPECIFICALLY, THE GOAL IS TO REFINE A MATHEMATICAL MODEL TO PREDICT THE RATE OF DNMT3AKO CLONAL EXPANSION DURING INFECTION. WE WILL APPLY OUR MEASUREMENTS OF HSC DIVISION, DEATH, DIFFERENTIATION AND SIZE OF THE DNMT3A-MUTANT HSC POPULATION (AIMS 2.1, 2.2) TO OPTIMIZE THE MATHEMATICAL MODEL OF CLONAL DYNAMICS DURING INFECTION. ADDITIONAL DATA WILL ALLOW COMPUTATION OF CONFIDENCE INTERVALS FOR THE EXISTING MODELS AND A COMPARISON OF THE VARIABILITY OF THE DATA COMPARED TO THE STOCHASTIC SPREAD OF THE MODEL TRAJECTORIES. ADDITION OF FURTHER EXPERIMENTAL DATA DERIVED FROM AIM 2.2 WILL ADDRESS NOT ONLY WHETHER EXPERIMENTAL REPLICATES ADHERE TO THE MODEL BUT ALSO THE GENERALIZABILITY OF THE MODEL TO OTHER INFECTIOUS CONDITIONS. MODEL REFINEMENTS WILL INCLUDE A NONLINEAR IFNγ FEEDBACK FROM PERIPHERAL T CELLS (SIMILAR TO THAT IN STIEHL ET AL.50), WHICH MAY ACT DIFFERENTLY ON WT VS. KO HSCS AND HELP EXPLAIN THE DYNAMICAL EFFECTS IN FIG. 7. DR. KIMMEL OR HIS STUDENT WILL PRESENT RESULTS AT ONE NATIONAL CONFERENCE ANNUALLY.

subaward_description:
THE WORK CONDUCTED AT RICE UNIVERSITY WILL BE MANAGED BY RANDALL HULET. HIS RESPONSIBILITIES
ARE TO PROVIDE DAY TO DAY SUPERVISION OF THE UNDERGRADUATE, GRADUATE, AND POST-DOCTORAL SCIENTISTS
WHO ARE INVOLVED IN THIS PROJECT. HE WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR MAKING DECISIONS REGARDING THE
TECHNICAL APPROACH OF THE PART OF THE PROJECT CONDUCTED AT RICE, AND FOR THE CONTENT OF
PUBLICATIONS RESULTING FROM THIS WORK.
THERE WILL BE TWO EXPERIMENTAL THRUSTS AT RICE UNIVERSITY, ONE WITH 3D FERMIONS AND THE OTHER
WITH 1D FERMIONS. BOTH EXPERIMENTS UTILIZE 6LI, A FERMIONIC ATOM. THE 3D EXPERIMENT SEEKS TO
MODEL THE SUPPRESSION OF PARTICLE JETS PRODUCED IN THE QUARK-GLUON PLASMA USING A UNITARY FERMI
GAS WITH TWO SPIN-STATES. BY MEASURING THE TRANSMISSION OF ATOMS PREPARED IN A SINGLE SPIN-STATE,
WE WILL LEARN ABOUT THE LOW AND HIGH-MOMENTUM PARTS OF THE INTERACTION.
THE OTHER THRUST INVOLVES PREPARING ATOMS IN A TWO-DIMENSIONAL OPTICAL LATTICE, WHICH RESULTS IN
AN ARRAY OF 1D TUBES. OUR GOAL IS TO DETECT AND CHARACTERIZE SPIN-CHARGE SEPARATION, A PARADIGM
OF STRONGLY CORRELATED MATTER IN LOW-DIMENSIONS. A RELATED EXPERIMENT WILL BE WEAKEN THE LATTICE
IN ORDER THAT THE SYSTEM CROSSES OVER FROM A 1D INTEGRABLE SYSTEM INTO EITHER A 2D OR 3D NONINTEGRABLE
ONE AND TO STUDY HOW THE DYNAMICS OF PARTICLE THERMALIZATION IS MODIFIED.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
"THIS RESEARCH DEVELOPS INNOVATIVE REDUCTION APPROACHES FOR HIGH-DIMENSIONAL MODELS OF RELEVANCE
TO HYPERSONIC FLIGHT; ADVANCED PARTITIONED PROCEDURES FOR THE ACCURATE AND FAST SOLUTION
OF COUPLED PROBLEMS; MULTI-FIDELITY MODELING APPROACHES GROUNDED IN TRUST-REGION THEORY; FASTER
OPTIMIZATION ALGORITHMS; INNOVATIVE UNCERTAINTY QUANTIFICATION METHODS FOR REDUCED-ORDER MODELS;
VERIFIED AND COMPUTATIONALLY TRACTABLE FRAMEWORK FOR TIGHTLY-COUPLED MULTI-PHYSICS ANALYSIS AND OPTIMIZATION
(MDAO), UNDER UNCERTAINTY, OF HYPERSONIC SYSTEMS UNDER UNCERTAINTY; DEMONSTRATION OF THE
POTENTIAL OF THIS FRAMEWORK WITH THE DESIGN OPTIMIZATION OF A GENERIC HYPERSONIC BOOST-GLIDE VEHICLE;
BENCHMARK EXAMPLES AND OPEN-SOURCE CODES ON GITHUB; OUTREACH AND DISSEMINATION PROGRAM.
RICE WILL LEAD THE DEVELOPMENT OF OPTIMIZATION ALGORITHMS THAT INTEGRATE REDUCED ORDER MODELS
OR SURROGATE MODELS, TO ACHIEVE GLOBAL CONVERGENCE (CONVERGENCE FROM ANY INITIAL DESIGN) OF MDAO
PERFORMED UNDER UNCERTAINTY. MOREOVER, RICE WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THEORETICAL ANALYSES OF ALL ASPECTS
OF THE PROJECT, INCLUDING PROJECTION BASED REDUCED ORDER MODELS, EFFICIENT METHODS FOR UNCERTAINTY
QUANTIFICATION, AND CONTROL LAWS. PROFESSOR MATTHIAS HEINKENSCHLOSS WILL DIRECT THE RESEARCH AND WILL
SUPERVISE A GRADUATE STUDENT AND A POSTDOCTORAL ASSOCIATE."

subaward_description:
IN THIS ONE-YEAR PILOT GRANT, WE PROPOSE TO: (1) DEVELOP AND OPTIMIZE A LATERAL FLOW TEST TO DETECT
HPV DNA FROM CERVICAL CYTOLOGY SPECIMENS; (2) CHARACTERIZE THE PERFORMANCE OF OUR LATERAL
FLOW HPV TEST IN A LAB SETTING; AND (3) ASSESS USABILITY OF OUR LATERAL FLOW HPV DNA TEST BY
NURSES AND COMMUNITY HEALTH WORKERS IN EL SALVADOR. THE PROPOSED WORK WILL ALLOW US TO
OPTIMIZE SAMPLE PREPARATION METHODS, FULLY CHARACTERIZE TEST PERFORMANCE IN THE LAB AND
ASSESS USABILITY OF THE DEVICE IN A POINT-OF-CARE SETTING. RESULTS OF THIS WORK WILL ENABLE US TO
EFFICIENTLY IDENTIFY AND RESOLVE ANY IN-FIELD CHALLENGES BEFORE PROCEEDING TO SUBSEQUENT FIELD
EVALUATION STUDIES

HE WILL ALSO PARTICIPATE IN SOME OF THE FIELD WORK IN AND AROUND THE GREAK LAKES BASIN, INCLUDING COLLECTING FIELD SAMPLES FOR GENETIC SCREENING AND TESTING EDNA MARKERS IN THE FIELD FOR INVASIVE SPECIES DETECTION.. MOREOVER, HE WILL CONTINUE TO PARTICIPATE IN THE MANAGEMENT TRANSITION BOARD (MTB)MEETINGS.

prime_award_cfda_numbers_and_titles: 93.286: DISCOVERY AND APPLIED RESEARCH FOR TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATIONS TO IMPROVE HU; 93.286: DISCOVERY AND APPLIED RESEARCH FOR TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATIONS TO IMPROVE HUMAN HEALTH
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
1. PLANNED EXPERIMENTS AND PLAN FOR FOLLOW-ON FUNDING: TO ACHIEVE OUR AIMS, WE PROPOSE
THE FOLLOWING STUDIES:
(1) DEVELOP AND OPTIMIZE A LATERAL FLOW TEST TO DETECT HPV DNA FROM CERVICAL CYTOLOGY
SPECIMENS: OUR PRELIMINARY DATA HAS BEEN GENERATED USING DIGENE HC2 HRHPV PROBES. IN
OPTIMIZING OUR LATERAL FLOW TEST, OUR FIRST STEP WILL BE TO OPTIMIZE THE ASSAY WITH COMMERCIALLY
AVAILABLE HPV DNA AND RNA PROBES FOR ALL HIGH-RISK HPV TYPES. WE WILL OPTIMIZE PROBE
SELECTION TO ENSURE THAT WE ACHIEVE THE INTENDED LIMIT OF DETECTION. IN TANDEM, WE WILL DEVELOP
SAMPLE PREPARATION METHODS SO THAT THE TEST CAN BE PERFORMED USING CELLS COLLECTED WITH A
TRADITIONAL CYTOLOGY BRUSH. WE ENVISION THAT THE BRUSH WILL BE PLACED IMMEDIATELY IN A VIAL OF
LYSIS BUFFER THAT PRESERVES DNA. WE WILL CREATE A LOW-COST BATTERY-POWERED HEATER, CAPABLE OF
PERFORMING THE HYBRIDIZATION STEPS IN THIS VIAL IN A LOW-RESOURCE SETTING. WE PLAN TO USE A LOWCOST
TRANSFER PIPETTE TO PLACE A DROP OF TREATED SAMPLE ON THE LATERAL FLOW TEST. WE WILL ASSESS
THE STABILITY OF OUR TEST AND THE DRIED REAGENT PADS IN HIGH HUMIDITY AND TEMPERATURE SETTINGS.
WE HAVE PREVIOUSLY USED DRIED STORAGE FOR THE DIAMINOBENZIDENE COLORIMETRIC REACTION USING A
MALARIA-BASED PROTEIN WITH OUR PAPER-BASED PLATFORM.
(2) CHARACTERIZE THE PERFORMANCE OF OUR LATERAL FLOW HPV TEST IN A LAB SETTING: WE THEN PLAN TO
TEST OUR LATERAL FLOW DEVICE USING HPV16 POSITIVE CELL LINES (SIHA AND CASKI) AND HPV 18
POSITIVE CELLS LINES (HELA) AS WELL AS HPV NEGATIVE CELL LINES. WE WILL PREPARE MIXTURES OF HPV
NEGATIVE CELLS (C33A) WITH HPV POSITIVE CELLS TO MIMIC CERVICAL CYTOLOGY SPECIMENS. WE WILL
DETERMINE THE MINIMAL AMOUNT OF SAMPLE PREPARATION NEEDED IN A LAB-BASED SETTING TO ACHIEVE
THE INTENDED LIMIT OF DETECTION STARTING WITH THE ESTIMATED NUMBER OF CELLS HARVESTED ON A
CYTOLOGY BRUSH. WE WILL OPTIMIZE THE TEST AS NEEDED TO ACHIEVE THE LIMIT OF DETECTION UNDER
TYPICAL CONDITIONS THAT MAY BE ENCOUNTERED IN LOW-RESOURCE SETTINGS. FINALLY, WE WILL TEST ASSAY
PERFORMANCE USING PATIENT SAMPLES OBTAINED FROM 20 PATIENTS IN CONJUNCTION WITH OUR
COLLABORATORS AT THE MD ANDERSON CANCER CENTER. WE WILL PERFORM OUR TEST USING CELLS THAT
WOULD ORDINARILY BE DISCARDED FOLLOWING HPV TESTING FOR STANDARD OF CARE. RESULTS OF OUR TEST WILL
BE COMPARED TO THAT OF THE DIGENE HC2 TEST.
(3) ASSESS USABILITY OF OUR LATERAL FLOW HPV DNA TEST BY NURSES LAB TECHNICIANS IN EL SALVADOR:
LASTLY, WE WILL PERFORM A SMALL USABILITY STUDY IN EL SALVADOR. WE WILL ASK A GROUP OF 10 NURSES
AND 10 LAB TECHNICIANS TO PERFORM THE TEST USING MOCK SAMPLES THAT INCLUDE HPV POSITIVE AND
NEGATIVE CELL LINES. WE WILL PROVIDE USERS WITH A SHORT TRAINING AND DEMONSTRATION OF HOW TO
PERFORM THE TEST. THEY WILL THEN BE ASKED TO PERFORM THE TEST INDEPENDENTLY USING MOCK SAMPLES.
WE WILL MONITOR SUBJECTS AS THEY PERFORM THE TEST TO NOTE ANY DEVIATION FROM TEST DIRECTIONS.
SUBJECTS WILL BE INTERVIEWED FOLLOWING THE STUDY TO IDENTIFY USABILITY CHALLENGES AND POSSIBLE
IMPROVEMENTS TO MAKE THE TEST EASIER TO USE. THE PILOT STUDY IN EL SALVADOR WILL HELP US IDENTIFY
AND UNDERSTAND ANY LIMITATIONS WITH SAMPLE PREPARATION OR USABILITY AT THE POINT-OF-CARE. AFTER
USING THIS VALUABLE INPUT TO MAKE NECESSARY CHANGES TO OUR DESIGN, WE WILL CONSULT WITH CFTCC
CENTER LEADERSHIP TO HELP IDENTIFY CLINICAL SITES FOR LARGER SCALE FIELD EVALUATION AND TO
SUBSEQUENTLY IDENTIFY COMMERCIAL PARTNERS FOR TRANSLATION.

THIS WILL INVOLVE MONTHLY MEETINGS AND PRESENTATION OF QUARTERLY FINDINGS. ALL INVESTIGATORS WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE TRAINING CORE BY ENGAGING IN APPROPRIATE DISSEMINATION ACTIVITIES AND SUPPORTING THE DESIGN AND DELIVERY OF WEBINARS AND OTHER TRAINING ACTIVITIES IN LINE WITH THEIR RESPECTIVE ROLES ON THE PROJECT.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: CENTER FOR THE COMPUTATIONAL DESIGN OF FUNCTIONAL LAYERED MATERIALS
prime_award_cfda_numbers_and_titles: 81.049: OFFICE OF SCIENCE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OBJECTIVES: LAYERED MATERIALS ARE TYPICALLY COMPOSED OF PLANAR SHEETS, WITH
STRONG INTERATOMIC BONDING WITHIN A LAYER AND WEAK BINDING BETWEEN LAYERS. A
COLLECTION OF MANY LAYERS IS A THREE-DIMENSIONAL (3D OR BULK) MATERIAL, WHILE ONE
OR A FEW LAYERS MAKE A 2D MATERIAL. SEMICONDUCTING LAYERED MATERIALS (E.G., MOS2)
OF EITHER DIMENSIONALITY HAVE USEFUL FUNCTIONALITIES FOR MANY POTENTIAL APPLICATIONS,
AND CAN BE MODIFIED FOR SPECIFIC PURPOSES BY VARIOUS DEFECTS AND PERTURBATIONS.
THE MAIN OBJECTIVE OF THE CENTER IS TO EMPLOY COMPUTATION AND THEORY TO DESIGN
MODIFIED LAYERED MATERIALS WITH DESIRED FUNCTIONALITIES, TO GROW AND EXPERIMENTALLY
CHARACTERIZE THEM, AND TO TEST THEIR EFFICACY FOR CLEAN-ENERGY APPLICATIONS.
DESCRIPTION OF THE PROJECT: THE CENTER HAS FIVE INTERCONNECTED THRUSTS: (1) FIRSTPRINCIPLES
DENSITY-FUNCTIONAL AND CORRELATED-WAVEFUNCTION APPROACHES WILL BE
DEVELOPED, WITH IMPROVED ACCURACY FOR STRONG AND WEAK BONDS, AND FOR NORMAL AND
STRONG ELECTRON CORRELATIONS. (2) FIRST-PRINCIPLES CALCULATIONS WILL BE MADE FOR
PRISTINE AND DEFECTED LAYERED MATERIALS PLUS ARTIFICIAL HETEROSTRUCTURES UNDER THE
INFLUENCE OF STRAIN & EXTERNAL FIELDS; THE DEFECTS WILL INCLUDE VACANCIES, INTERCALATED
IONS, ADSORBED ATOMS, AND MOLECULES. (3) MULTI-SCALE MODELS USING FIRST-PRINCIPLES
INPUT WILL BE DEVELOPED FOR EXTENDED DEFECTS. (4) CANDIDATE MATERIALS WILL BE
GROWN AND EXPERIMENTALLY CHARACTERIZED. (5) CATALYSIS BY LAYERED MATERIALS OF
WATER SPLITTING, AND CONVERSION OF CO2 TO METHANOL, WILL BE STUDIED AND OPTIMIZED.
POTENTIAL IMPACT/BENEFITS OF THE PROJECT: OUR BASIC-SCIENCE CENTER WILL YIELD
IMPROVED ELECTRONIC-STRUCTURE METHODOLOGIES TO DIRECTLY IMPACT MUCH OF MATERIALS
SCIENCE, CONDENSED MATTER PHYSICS AND CHEMISTRY, PLUS OTHER WIDE-RANGING
MATERIALS-BY-DESIGN AND MATERIALS GENOME INITIATIVES. THE LONG-RANGE GOAL OF THE
EFRC IS TO DESIGN MATERIALS PLATFORMS FOR BATTERIES, PHOTOVOLTAICS FOR SOLAR ENERGY,
AND PHOTOCATALYSTS TO GENERATE CLEAN HYDROGEN FUEL FROM WATER.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description: WE WILL CONDUCT MONTE CARLO SIMULATIONS OF FPDEÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S UNDER WHITE AND COLORED NOISE. IN THIS REGARD THE ISSUE OF STABILITY OF NUMERICAL ALGORITHMS FOR SOLVING FPDE WILL BE SEPARATED FROM THAT WHICH PERTAINS TO A BOUND-INPUT‐BOUND‐OUTPUT (BIO) STABILITY PERSPECTIVE. SEVERAL NON‐WHITE SPECTRA WILL BE CONSIDERED IN AN ATTEMPT TO REPRESENT EXCITATIONS OF FPDES ENCOUNTERED IN PHYSICAL SETTINGS. APPLICATIONS PERTAINING TO VARIOUS CONTINUA SUCH AS BEAMS AND PLATES WILL BE CONSIDERED. A PARTICULARLY EFFICIENT ALGORITHM FOR SOLVING LINEAR AND NON‐LINEAR FPDS NUMERICALLY WILL BE USED FOR THE MONTE CARLO SIMULATION, CANVASSING OF EXISTING METHODS FOR EXAMINING BIBO RELATED STABILITY ISSUES OF ORDINARY FDES WILL BE UNDERTAKE AS A PRELUDE TO ACQUIRING EXPERIENCE AND DEVELOPING CRITERIA FOR STOCHASTICALLY FORCED FPDES.

prime_award_funding_agency_name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)
prime_awardee_business_types: Public/State Controlled Institution of Higher Education
prime_award_base_transaction_description: WE PROPOSE TO INVESTIGATE AND CONTRAST THE EFFECTS OF STOCHASTIC (DIFFUSIVE) TRANSPORT PROCESSES WITH TRANSPORT RESULTING FROM COHERENT INTERACTIONS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES WITH LARGE SCALE MAGNETOSPHERIC DISTURBANCES. PROCESSES AFFECTING THE DYNAMICS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE MAY BE BROADLY CATEGORIZED AS EITHER STOCHASTIC PROCESSES  DESCRIBING INDIVIDUALLY-RANDOM DYNAMICS OF AN ENSEMBLE OF PARTICLES INTERACTING WITH A SPECTRUM OF MAGNETOSPHERIC WAVES; OR COHERENT PROCESSES  WHEREBY THE DYNAMICS OF THE PARTICLES ARE COLLECTIVELY DRIVEN IN ONE DIRECTION IN ENERGY OR SPACE. ON TIMESCALES LONGER THAN THE DRIFT PERIOD STOCHASTIC PROCESSES LEAD TO RADIAL DIFFUSION  WHEREBY PARTICLES MOVE RANDOMLY THROUGH REGIONS OF HIGHER OR LOWER MAGNETIC FIELD STRENGTH  GAINING OR LOSING ENERGY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONSERVATION OF THE PARTICLE'S MAGNETIC MOMENT. THE RADIAL PROFILE OF PHASE SPACE DENSITY DETERMINES WHETHER A NET INCREASE IN ENERGY OCCURS RESULTING FROM A POSITIVE OUTWARD GRADIENT OR A NET LOSS OF ENERGY FROM A NEGATIVE GRADIENT. COHERENT PROCESSES  BY CONTRAST  RESULT FROM INTERACTIONS WITH CONVECTIVE OR IMPULSIVE TRANSFERS OF ENERGY WITH A WELL-DEFINED DIRECTION. EXAMPLES OF KEY COHERENT PROCESSES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE INCLUDE PLASMASHEET INJECTIONS INTO THE STABLE TRAPPING REGION DURING SUBSTORMS  AND INJECTIONS OF PREVIOUSLY-TRAPPED PARTICLES FROM HIGH L VALUES DURING IMPULSIVE EVENTS INDUCED BY SHOCKS IN THE SOLAR WIND.
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description: THIS THREE-YEAR SUBCONTRACT IS IN SUPPORT OF THE NASA PROPOSAL ENTITLED THE IMPORTANCE OF EVENT-SPECIFIC SPATIAL AND TEMPORAL VARIATIONS IN CHORUS WAVE ACTIVITY ON RADIATION BELT EVOLUTION, UNDER THE DIRECTION OF PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR DOCTOR ALLISON JAYNES OF THE UNIVERSITY OF IOWA. DR. CHANÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITY IN THIS PROJECT WILL BE TO ASSIST DR. SCOT ELKINGTON WITH COMPUTER SIMULATIONS USING THE STOCHASTIC MHD/PARTICLE CODE K2 TO MODEL TRAPPED PARTICLE DYNAMICS DURING PERIODS OF GEOMAGNETIC ACTIVITY. IN PARTICULAR, OVER THE COURSE OF THIS PROJECT DR. CHAN WILL:- DETERMINE THE DIFFUSION COEFFICIENTS APPROPRIATE FOR EACH STORM EVENT THAT WE WISH TO MODEL,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE TESTING AND VALIDATION OF THE SDE METHOD IN THE CONTEXT OF THESE SIMULATIONS,- ASSIST IN THE INTERPRETATION OF THE SIMULATION RESULTS, INCLUDING COMPARISONS WITH VAN ALLEN PROBE SPACECRAFT DATA,- ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE IN EFFORTS TO REPORT THE RESULTS FROM THIS PROJECT TO SCIENTIFIC JOURNALS ANDCONFERENCES AND,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE ANNUAL REPORT FOR THE PROJECT.

SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE

ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

subaward_description:
OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

subaward_description:
EXPEDITION 392 STUDY OF SULPHUR CYCLING IN AGULHAS PLATEAU SEDIMENTS: DEBADRITA JANA CONTRIBUTION
THE LISTED PI, DR. MARK TORRES, IS JANAÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S ADVISOR AT RICE UNIVERSITY AND WILL OVERSEE THIS WORK. THE DYNAMICS OF THE GLOBAL SULFUR CYCLE AFFECT BOTH THE ACID/BASE AND
REDUCTION/OXIDATION CHEMISTRY OF THE OCEAN-ATMOSPHERE SYSTEM (VAN DE VELDE ER AL., 2020) WITH IMPLICATIONS FOR THE EVOLUTIONS BOTH ATMOSPHERIC PCO2 AND PO2 OVER GEOLOGIC
TIMESCALES (TORRES ET AL., 2014; BERNER, 1984). CHANGES IN THE GLOBAL S CYCLE ARE TYPICALLY RECONSTRUCTED USING SULFUR ISOTOPIC RATIOS GIVEN THE STRONG FRACTIONATION INDUCED BY
MICROBIAL SULFATE REDUCTION AND THE INCORPORATION OF THIS REDUCED SULFUR INTO SEDIMENTARY PYRITE (RAISEWELL, 1997). ACCORDINGLY, THE ÃƑÂ´Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Å¾Ã‚Â¯34S VALUE OF THE OCEAN IS THOUGHT TO REFLECT
THE RELATIVE AMOUNTS OF SULFATE VERSUS PYRITE BURIAL (FIKE ET AL., 2015). QUANTITATIVE INTERPRETATIONS OF CHANGES IN MARINE ÃƑÂ´Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Å¾Ã‚Â¯34S VALUES IN TERMS OF THE GLOBAL CARBON AND OXYGEN
CYCLES THUS REQUIRE KNOWLEDGE OF THE INTEGRATED ISOTOPIC COMPOSITION OF BURIED PYRITE. MUCH PAST WORK HAS CONSIDERED A SINGLE EFFECTIVE FRACTIONATION FACTOR DESCRIBING THE ISOTOPIC
CONSEQUENCES OF PYRITE BURIAL (CANFIELD ET AL., 1996; FIKE ET AL., 2006; OWENS ET AL., 2013). HOWEVER, RECENT STUDIES SUGGEST THAT THERE ARE STRONG LOCAL CONTROLS ON ÃƑÂ´Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Å¾Ã‚Â¯34S OF
MARINE PYRITES, SUCH AS DEPOSITIONAL SETTING (PASQUIER ET AL., 2021), BIOTURBATION (SCHREIBER ET AL., 2002; VIRTASALO ET AL., 2013; HARAZIM ET AL., 2015, 2020), AND MICROBIAL DIVERSITY
(KOHN ET AL., 1998). COMPLEX MICRO-SCALE CONTROLS RESULT IN THE FORMATION OF PYRITES HAVING DIFFERENT MORPHOLOGIES AND ISOTOPIC SIGNATURES THAT CANNOT BE WELL-DESCRIBED BY A
SINGLE FRACTIONATION FACTOR. POTENTIALLY, CHANGES IN THE RELATIVE CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE VARIOUS PYRITE FORMING MECHANISMS COULD MODIFY THE INTEGRATED S ISOTOPIC COMPOSITION OF BURIED
PYRITE MAKING IT DIFFICULT TO RECONSTRUCT GLOBAL C AND O CYCLE DYNAMICS WITHOUT A MECHANISTIC UNDERSTANDING OF THE LOCAL CONTROLS ON PYRITE ÃƑÂ´Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Å¾Ã‚Â¯34S.
HERE, WE PROPOSE TO INVESTIGATE HOW MULTIPLE PYRITE-FORMING MECHANISMS CONTROL S ISOTOPIC FRACTIONATION USING SEDIMENTARY PYRITES FROM LATE CRETACEOUS ORGANIC-RICH,
BIOTURBATED CLAYSTONES RECOVERED AT IODP SITE 1581(TRANSKEI BASIN). THE TARGET SEDIMENT INTERVALS CONSIST OF DARK GREENISH GRAY SANDSTONES AND SILTSTONES, AND DARK GREENISH
GRAY TO BLACK CLAYSTONES. SHIPBOARD AGE MODELS SUGGEST THAT THE SEDIMENTS ARE OF MAASTRICHTIAN TO CAMPANIAN AGE. PYRITE IS ABUNDANT THROUGHOUT THESE SEDIMENTS AND OCCURS
AS THREE DIFFERENT TEXTURES THAT WE INFER TO REFLECT DIFFERENT FORMATION MECHANISMS: (1) INFILLING WITHIN SEVERAL MM- TO CM-SCALE BURROWS, (2) NODULES WITH SEVERAL MM- TO CM-WIDE
DIAMETER, AND (3) SMALLER, DISSEMINATED SUB-MM- TO MM-SCALE PATCHES OR FRAMBOIDS. THESE SAMPLES ARE FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY LOW CARBONATE CONTENT (<5 WT %) AND HIGH
ORGANIC CARBON CONTENT (0.5- 1.5 WT. %), SUGGESTING AN OXYGEN-POOR ENVIRONMENT IDEAL FOR PYRITE FORMATION AND PRESERVATION. MARINE SEDIMENTARY PYRITE FORMATION INCREASES
DURING PERIODS IN EARTH HISTORY CHARACTERIZED BY HIGH PRODUCTIVITY AND ORGANIC MATTER RESPIRATION IN THE SURFACE-UPPER OCEAN, ENHANCED ANOXIA, AND ORGANIC MATTER PRESERVATION AT
DEPTH; NOTABLY THE CRETACEOUS OCEAN ANOXIC EVENTS (OAES) (JENKYNS, 2010). ALTHOUGH SEDIMENTS OF CENOMANIAN-TURONIAN AGE (CORRESPONDING TO OAE2) WERE NOT REACHED
WHILE DRILLING SITE 1581, THE ORGANIC MATTER CONTENT AND OCCURRENCE OF PYRITE IN THESE YOUNGER SEDIMENTS SUGGEST THAT THEY CAN BE USEFUL IN STUDYING THE BIOGEOCHEMICAL CYCLING OF
C, S, O, FE, AND OTHER TRACE METALS DURING THE LATE CRETACEOUS.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: FRACTIONAL PDES FOR CONSERVATION LAWS AND BEYOND: THEORY, NUMERICS AND APPLICATIONS
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OUR OVERARCHING GOAL IS THE DEVELOPMENT OF A SEAMLESS INTEGRATION OF THEORY AND ALGORITHMS FOR DATA-DRIVEN FPDES, WHILE TARGETING THE COMMON THEMES OF CONSERVATION, MONOTONICITY, ADAPTIVE REFINEMENT AND HIGHORDER ACCURACY IN THE CONTEXT OF SIMULATIONS OF MULTIPHASE FLOWS, TURBULENCE, FLUID-STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS, HYDROLOGY AND SOLID MECHANICS. MORE SPECIFICALLY, WE ENVISION A COMBINED MATHEMATICAL COMPUTATIONAL FRAMEWORK WHERE THE FRACTIONAL OPERATORS CAN BE CONSTRUCTED BASED ON AVAILABLE DATA, HENCE PROVIDING MODEL VALIDATION FOR THE SPECIFIC PHENOMENON BUT ALSO A PROBLEM-BASED SIMULATION CAPABILITY THAT CAN BE SCALED TO REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS. THE PROPOSED RESEARCH PLAN EMPHASIZES A DYNAMIC INTEGRATION OF FIVE MAIN RESEARCH AREAS THAT FORM THE PILLARS OF OUR COMPREHENSIVE INTEGRATED APPROACH:
RESEARCH AREA 1: THEORY OF NON-LOCAL OPERATORS AND STOCHASTIC INTERPRETATION OF FRACTIONAL OPERATORS.
RESEARCH AREA 2: NUMERICAL SOLUTION OF FPDES WITH EMPHASIS ON VARIABLE FRACTIONAL ORDER.
RESEARCH AREA 3: FAST SOLVERS FOR DENSE LINEAR SYSTEMS WITH APPROXIMATELY LINEAR COMPUTATIONAL COMPLEXITY.
RESEARCH AREA 4: STOCHASTIC FPDES AND DETERMINATION OF THE FRACTIONAL ORDER DIRECTLY BY AVAILABLE DATA.
RESEARCH AREA 5: APPLICATION OF THE INTEGRATED FRAMEWORK TO FLUID AND SOLID MECHANICS PROBLEMS, FLUID-STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS, TWO-PHASE FLOWS AND REACTIVE SYSTEMS WITH FRONT PROPAGATION.

subaward_description: LAYERED MATERIALS ARE TYPICALLY COMPOSED OF PLANAR SHEETS, WITH STRONG INTERATOMIC BONDING WITHIN A LAYER AND WEAK BINDING BETWEEN LAYERS. A COLLECTION OF MANY LAYERS IS A THREE-DIMENSIONAL (3D OR BULK) MATERIAL, WHILE ONE OR A FEW LAYERS MAKE A 2D MATERIAL. SEMICONDUCTING LAYERED MATERIALS (E.G., MOS2) OF EITHER DIMENSIONALITY HAVE USEFUL FUNCTIONALITIES FOR MANY POTENTIAL APPLICATIONS, AND CAN BE MODIFIED FOR SPECIFIC PURPOSES BY VARIOUS DEFECTS AND PERTURBATIONS. THE MAIN OBJECTIVE OF THE CENTER IS TO EMPLOY COMPUTATION AND THEORY TO DESIGN MODIFIED LAYERED MATERIALS WITH DESIRED FUNCTIONALITIES, TO GROW AND EXPERIMENTALLY CHARACTERIZE THEM, AND TO TEST THEIR EFFICACY FOR CLEAN-ENERGY APPLICATIONS. DESCRIPTION OF THE PROJECT: THE CENTER HAS FIVE INTERCONNECTED THRUSTS: (1) FIRSTPRINCIPLES DENSITY-FUNCTIONAL AND CORRELATED-WAVEFUNCTION APPROACHES WILL BE DEVELOPED, WITH IMPROVED ACCURACY FOR STRONG AND WEAK BONDS, AND FOR NORMAL AND STRONG ELECTRON CORRELATIONS. (2) FIRST-PRINCIPLES CALCULATIONS WILL BE MADE FOR PRISTINE AND DEFECTED LAYERED MATERIALS PLUS ARTIFICIAL HETEROSTRUCTURES UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF STRAIN & EXTERNAL FIELDS; THE DEFECTS WILL INCLUDE VACANCIES, INTERCALATED IONS, ADSORBED ATOMS, AND MOLECULES. (3) MULTI-SCALE MODELS USING FIRST-PRINCIPLES INPUT WILL BE DEVELOPED FOR EXTENDED DEFECTS. (4) CANDIDATE MATERIALS WILL BE GROWN AND EXPERIMENTALLY CHARACTERIZED. (5) CATALYSIS BY LAYERED MATERIALS OF WATER SPLITTING, AND CONVERSION OF CO2 TO METHANOL, WILL BE STUDIED AND OPTIMIZED. POTENTIAL IMPACT/BENEFITS OF THE PROJECT: OUR BASIC-SCIENCE CENTER WILL YIELD IMPROVED ELECTRONIC-STRUCTURE METHODOLOGIES TO DIRECTLY IMPACT MUCH OF MATERIALS SCIENCE, CONDENSED MATTER PHYSICS AND CHEMISTRY, PLUS OTHER WIDE-RANGING MATERIALS-BY-DESIGN AND MATERIALS GENOME INITIATIVES. THE LONG-RANGE GOAL OF THE EFRC IS TO DESIGN MATERIALS PLATFORMS FOR BATTERIES, PHOTOVOLTAICS FOR SOLAR ENERGY, AND PHOTOCATALYSTS TO GENERATE CLEAN HYDROGEN FUEL FROM WATER

subaward_description:
TO CONSTRUCT A FIRM FOUNDATION FOR FURTHER STUDY, THE WORK PROPOSED HERE WILL FOCUS ON SAMPLES THAT WERE SHOWN TO BE BEST
BEHAVED UNDER SHIPBOARD ANALYSIS (CORES U1578-20R THROUGH -32R AND CORES U1578-48R THROUGH -65R). THIS WORK WILL TEST
WHETHER MORE PRECISE RESULTS CAN BE OBTAINED IN THE LAB ENVIRONMENT (RELATIVE TO THE SHIPBOARD LAB), IF THE MAGNETIC CARRIERS OF
THESE ROCKS ARE MORE STABLE UNDER AF OR THERMAL TREATMENT, TO REFINE COOLING UNIT ESTIMATES, AND TO BETTER DETERMINE BOUNDARIES OF
GEOMAGNETIC REVERSALS. SHIPBOARD PALEOMAGNETIC RESULTS IDENTIFIED AT LEAST ONE CLEAR REVERSAL (AND POSSIBLY TWO), SO ONE GOAL OF
THIS EARLY STUDY WILL BE TO REFINE PALEOMAGNETIC RESULTS AROUND THESE REVERSALS AND DETERMINE THEIR RELIABILITY. THIS WILL HELP US
UNDERSTAND THE AMOUNT OF GEOLOGIC TIME CONTAINED WITHIN THE RECOVERED VOLCANIC SUCCESSION AT SITE U1578. IN ADDITION, THE
NUMBER OF COOLING UNITS CONTAINED IN THE SUCCESSION IS GREATER THAN THE NUMBER OF INDEPENDENT READINGS OF THE GEOMAGNETIC FIELD
(BECAUSE MORE THAN ONE LAVA FLOW CAN BE ERUPTED IN A SHORT TIME) AND THUS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE SIZE OF THE UNCERTAINTY IN
PALEOLATITUDE. THEREFORE, DETERMINING THE NUMBER OF INDEPENDENT COOLING UNITS IN THESE SECTIONS WITH GREATER NUMBERS OF DATA WILL
HELP US UNDERSTAND THE RESOLUTION THAT CAN BE EXPECTED WITH FURTHER STUDY. THIS WILL BE CRITICAL TO CONVINCING REVIEWERS FOR ADDITIONAL
FUNDING. WHILE NOT ALL SAMPLES TAKEN IN THE MORE MAGNETICALLY WELL BEHAVED CORES WILL BE MEASURED IN THIS PRELIMINARY WORK, INITIAL
AVERAGE PALEOLATITUDES FROM THESE CORES WILL GUIDE OUR STUDY OF MORE MAGNETICALLY DIFFICULT PARTS OF THE SUCCESSION.
FUTURE WORK WILL FOCUS ON REGIONS WHICH PRESENTED MORE PALEOMAGNETIC COMPLEXITY DURING SHIPBOARD ANALYSIS. IN PARTICULAR, THE
INTERCALATED SEDIMENTARY UNITS AND A LARGE PILLOW SUCCESSION THE FORMER OF WHICH MAY REQUIRE A MORE SENSITIVE MAGNETOMETER THAN
THE JR-6 AVAILABLE TO US AT THE UNIVERSITY OF HOUSTON AND THE LATTER OF WHICH MAY REQUIRE MORE ROCK MAGNETIC CHARACTERIZATION TO
DETERMINE A PRODUCTIVE APPROACH FOR MEASUREMENT.

OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: WE PROPOSE TO INVESTIGATE AND CONTRAST THE EFFECTS OF STOCHASTIC (DIFFUSIVE) TRANSPORT PROCESSES WITH TRANSPORT RESULTING FROM COHERENT INTERACTIONS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES WITH LARGE SCALE MAGNETOSPHERIC DISTURBANCES. PROCESSES AFFECTING THE DYNAMICS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE MAY BE BROADLY CATEGORIZED AS EITHER STOCHASTIC PROCESSES  DESCRIBING INDIVIDUALLY-RANDOM DYNAMICS OF AN ENSEMBLE OF PARTICLES INTERACTING WITH A SPECTRUM OF MAGNETOSPHERIC WAVES; OR COHERENT PROCESSES  WHEREBY THE DYNAMICS OF THE PARTICLES ARE COLLECTIVELY DRIVEN IN ONE DIRECTION IN ENERGY OR SPACE. ON TIMESCALES LONGER THAN THE DRIFT PERIOD STOCHASTIC PROCESSES LEAD TO RADIAL DIFFUSION  WHEREBY PARTICLES MOVE RANDOMLY THROUGH REGIONS OF HIGHER OR LOWER MAGNETIC FIELD STRENGTH  GAINING OR LOSING ENERGY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONSERVATION OF THE PARTICLE'S MAGNETIC MOMENT. THE RADIAL PROFILE OF PHASE SPACE DENSITY DETERMINES WHETHER A NET INCREASE IN ENERGY OCCURS RESULTING FROM A POSITIVE OUTWARD GRADIENT OR A NET LOSS OF ENERGY FROM A NEGATIVE GRADIENT. COHERENT PROCESSES  BY CONTRAST  RESULT FROM INTERACTIONS WITH CONVECTIVE OR IMPULSIVE TRANSFERS OF ENERGY WITH A WELL-DEFINED DIRECTION. EXAMPLES OF KEY COHERENT PROCESSES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE INCLUDE PLASMASHEET INJECTIONS INTO THE STABLE TRAPPING REGION DURING SUBSTORMS  AND INJECTIONS OF PREVIOUSLY-TRAPPED PARTICLES FROM HIGH L VALUES DURING IMPULSIVE EVENTS INDUCED BY SHOCKS IN THE SOLAR WIND.

DR. CHANÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITY IN THIS PROJECT WILL BE TO ASSIST WITH COMPUTER SIMULATIONS USING THE STOCHASTIC MHD/PARTICLE CODE K2 TO MODEL TRAPPED PARTICLE DYNAMICS DURING PERIODS OF GEOMAGNETIC ACTIVITY. IN PARTICULAR, OVER THE COURSE OF THIS PROJECT RICE UNIVERSITY WILL:- DETERMINE THE DIFFUSION COEFFICIENTS APPROPRIATE FOR EACH STORM EVENT THAT WE WISH TO MODEL,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE TESTING AND VALIDATION OF THE SDE METHOD IN THE CONTEXT OF THESE SIMULATIONS,- ASSIST IN THE INTERPRETATION OF THE SIMULATION RESULTS, INCLUDING COMPARISONS WITH VAN ALLEN PROBE SPACECRAFT DATA,- ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE IN EFFORTS TO REPORT THE RESULTS FROM THIS PROJECT TO SCIENTIFIC JOURNALS ANDCONFERENCES AND,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE ANNUAL REPORT FOR THE PROJECT.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: Computational foundations of active visual sensing - Abstract Vision is an active process: we move our head and eyes to explore the sensory world. This is particularly important in situations where a stationary view provides limited information, such as when looking for an object that is occluded or obscured, which is common in complex natural scenes. However, our understanding of active vision is limited due to experimental and theoretical challenges, including the difculty of studying vision in freely moving animals and the lack of formal theoretical frameworks that integrate visual representations with actions. In this team project, we will combine expertise in visual neuroscience, behavior, machine learning, and theory, to determine the behavioral, neural, and computational underpinnings of active sensing. Our approach is based on a new theoretical framework of Bounded Rational Control (BRC), and a behavioral task in which mice perform an object recognition task in the presence of occlusion and image corruptions. To enable active sensing, stimuli in the task are rendered real-time in augmented reality based on the animal's viewpoint. In our rst aim, we will develop models of active sensing based on constrained visual representations in BRC. In the second aim, we measure behavioral performance (both correct/incorrect responses and full-body movements) during the task, and in the third aim we will measure neural activity across visual cortical areas during the task. For both Aims 2 and 3, we will t our models to the corresponding behavioral and neural data, and then perform causal tests of our models by presenting novel stimuli predicted to elicit specic responses from the model. Together, these aims will provide a foundational understanding of active vision in the mouse that will support a subsequent U19 proposal taking advantage of genetic tools to investigate the underlying local and long-range neural circuits.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: FRACTIONAL PDES FOR CONSERVATION LAWS AND BEYOND: THEORY, NUMERICS AND APPLICATIONS
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OUR OVERARCHING GOAL IS THE DEVELOPMENT OF A SEAMLESS INTEGRATION OF THEORY AND ALGORITHMS FOR DATA-DRIVEN FPDES, WHILE TARGETING THE COMMON THEMES OF CONSERVATION, MONOTONICITY, ADAPTIVE REFINEMENT AND HIGHORDER ACCURACY IN THE CONTEXT OF SIMULATIONS OF MULTIPHASE FLOWS, TURBULENCE, FLUID-STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS, HYDROLOGY AND SOLID MECHANICS. MORE SPECIFICALLY, WE ENVISION A COMBINED MATHEMATICAL COMPUTATIONAL FRAMEWORK WHERE THE FRACTIONAL OPERATORS CAN BE CONSTRUCTED BASED ON AVAILABLE DATA, HENCE PROVIDING MODEL VALIDATION FOR THE SPECIFIC PHENOMENON BUT ALSO A PROBLEM-BASED SIMULATION CAPABILITY THAT CAN BE SCALED TO REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS. THE PROPOSED RESEARCH PLAN EMPHASIZES A DYNAMIC INTEGRATION OF FIVE MAIN RESEARCH AREAS THAT FORM THE PILLARS OF OUR COMPREHENSIVE INTEGRATED APPROACH:
RESEARCH AREA 1: THEORY OF NON-LOCAL OPERATORS AND STOCHASTIC INTERPRETATION OF FRACTIONAL OPERATORS.
RESEARCH AREA 2: NUMERICAL SOLUTION OF FPDES WITH EMPHASIS ON VARIABLE FRACTIONAL ORDER.
RESEARCH AREA 3: FAST SOLVERS FOR DENSE LINEAR SYSTEMS WITH APPROXIMATELY LINEAR COMPUTATIONAL COMPLEXITY.
RESEARCH AREA 4: STOCHASTIC FPDES AND DETERMINATION OF THE FRACTIONAL ORDER DIRECTLY BY AVAILABLE DATA.
RESEARCH AREA 5: APPLICATION OF THE INTEGRATED FRAMEWORK TO FLUID AND SOLID MECHANICS PROBLEMS, FLUID-STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS, TWO-PHASE FLOWS AND REACTIVE SYSTEMS WITH FRONT PROPAGATION.

JAMES LONG WILL START A ONE-MONTH SUBCONTRACT EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2023, AND WILL WRITE CODE TO CALCULATE THE SPATIAL AND TEMPORAL COHERENCE FROM PHANTOM AND CLINICAL ECHO DATA ACQUIRED FROM VARIOUS ULTRASOUND SCANNERS. prime_award_base_transaction_description: WE PROPOSE TO INVESTIGATE AND CONTRAST THE EFFECTS OF STOCHASTIC (DIFFUSIVE) TRANSPORT PROCESSES WITH TRANSPORT RESULTING FROM COHERENT INTERACTIONS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES WITH LARGE SCALE MAGNETOSPHERIC DISTURBANCES. PROCESSES AFFECTING THE DYNAMICS OF ENERGETIC PARTICLES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE MAY BE BROADLY CATEGORIZED AS EITHER STOCHASTIC PROCESSES  DESCRIBING INDIVIDUALLY-RANDOM DYNAMICS OF AN ENSEMBLE OF PARTICLES INTERACTING WITH A SPECTRUM OF MAGNETOSPHERIC WAVES; OR COHERENT PROCESSES  WHEREBY THE DYNAMICS OF THE PARTICLES ARE COLLECTIVELY DRIVEN IN ONE DIRECTION IN ENERGY OR SPACE. ON TIMESCALES LONGER THAN THE DRIFT PERIOD STOCHASTIC PROCESSES LEAD TO RADIAL DIFFUSION  WHEREBY PARTICLES MOVE RANDOMLY THROUGH REGIONS OF HIGHER OR LOWER MAGNETIC FIELD STRENGTH  GAINING OR LOSING ENERGY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CONSERVATION OF THE PARTICLE'S MAGNETIC MOMENT. THE RADIAL PROFILE OF PHASE SPACE DENSITY DETERMINES WHETHER A NET INCREASE IN ENERGY OCCURS RESULTING FROM A POSITIVE OUTWARD GRADIENT OR A NET LOSS OF ENERGY FROM A NEGATIVE GRADIENT. COHERENT PROCESSES  BY CONTRAST  RESULT FROM INTERACTIONS WITH CONVECTIVE OR IMPULSIVE TRANSFERS OF ENERGY WITH A WELL-DEFINED DIRECTION. EXAMPLES OF KEY COHERENT PROCESSES IN THE INNER MAGNETOSPHERE INCLUDE PLASMASHEET INJECTIONS INTO THE STABLE TRAPPING REGION DURING SUBSTORMS  AND INJECTIONS OF PREVIOUSLY-TRAPPED PARTICLES FROM HIGH L VALUES DURING IMPULSIVE EVENTS INDUCED BY SHOCKS IN THE SOLAR WIND.

subaward_description: THIS THREE-YEAR SUBCONTRACT IS IN SUPPORT OF THE NASA PROPOSAL ENTITLED "THE IMPORTANCE OF EVENT-SPECIFIC SPATIAL AND TEMPORAL VARIATIONS IN CHORUS WAVE ACTIVITY ON RADIATION BELT EVOLUTION", UNDER THE DIRECTION OF PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR DOCTOR ALLISON JAYNES OF THE UNIVERSITY OF IOWA. PIÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITY IN THIS PROJECT WILL BE TO ASSIST CO-PI WITH COMPUTER SIMULATIONS USING THE STOCHASTIC MHD/PARTICLE CODE K2 TO MODEL TRAPPED PARTICLE DYNAMICS DURING PERIODS OF GEOMAGNETIC ACTIVITY. IN PARTICULAR, OVER THE COURSE OF THIS PROJECT PI WILL:- DETERMINE THE DIFFUSION COEFFICIENTS APPROPRIATE FOR EACH STORM EVENT THAT WE WISH TO MODEL,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE TESTING AND VALIDATION OF THE SDE METHOD IN THE CONTEXT OF THESE SIMULATIONS,- ASSIST IN THE INTERPRETATION OF THE SIMULATION RESULTS, INCLUDING COMPARISONS WITH VAN ALLEN PROBE SPACECRAFT DATA,- ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE IN EFFORTS TO REPORT THE RESULTS FROM THIS PROJECT TO SCIENTIFIC JOURNALS AND CONFERENCES AND,- CONTRIBUTE TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE ANNUAL REPORT FOR THE PROJECT.

subaward_description: UNDERSTANDING THE ORIGIN AND FATE OF METHANE IN SUBSEAFLOOR ENVIRONMENTS IS KEY TO UNDERSTANDING ITS ROLE IN CLIMATE CHANGE (1), THE EVOLUTION OF LIFE ON EARTH (2), AND THE POSSIBILITY OF LIFE ON OTHER OCEAN WORLDS (3). MATERIAL COLLECTED DURING EXP. 385 TO THE GUAYMAS BASIN ALLOWS AN UNPRECEDENTED GLIMPSE AT HOW METHANE IS CREATED, MOVES AND CONSUMED IN THE SUBSURFACE OF A YOUNG, RIFTING SYSTEM. WITHIN, I PROPOSE TO EXAMINE THIS PHENOMENON IN DETAIL: THE TRANSITION FROM SEDIMENTARY-ASSOCIATED METHANE TO SILL-ASSOCIATED METHANE AT SITE U1547 (HEREAFTER CALLED RING VENT). I WILL DETERMINE THE CLUMPED ISOTOPE COMPOSITION OF METHANE AND EXAMINE THE STABLE ISOTOPE COMPOSITION OF CALCITE AND PYRITE IN SILL MATERIAL IN ORDER TO EXPLORE THE EXCHANGE OF CARBON FROM THE ABIOTIC TO THE BIOGENIC REALM

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
OVERVIEW
THE LAMONT-DOHERTY EARTH OBSERVATORY (LDEO) OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY
SUPPORTS THE PARTICIPATION OF SCIENTISTS BASED AT U.S. INSTITUTIONS IN THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM (LOOP) THROUGH A COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FOR A U.S. SCIENCE SUPPORT PROGRAM (USSSP) ASSOCIATED WITH LOOP. THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.

THE IODP IS AN INTERNATIONAL MARINE RESEARCH DRILLING PROGRAM INVOLVING OVER 20 NATIONS COMMITTED TO ACHIEVING GOALS OUTLINED IN A 10-YEAR SCIENCE PLAN, ILLUMINATING EARTH'S PAST, PRESENT AND FUTURE: THE INTERNATIONAL OCEAN DISCOVERY PROGRAM SCIENCE PLAN FOR 20I 3-2023 (HTTP://WWW .IODP.ORG/TOPÃ‚Â­ RESOURCES/PROGRAM  -DOCUMENTS).

SELECTION AS A SCIENCE PARTY MEMBER OF AN LOOP EXPEDITION IS A PRESTIGIOUS
ACHIEVEMENT THAT PROVIDES THE SCIENTIST ACCESS TO EXTENSIVE RESEARCH
INFRASTRUCTURE. SCIENTISTS ALSO HAVE EXCLUSIVE ACCESS TO GEOLOGICAL SAMPLES AND DATA FOR ONE YEAR AFTER THE END OF THE EXPEDITION IN ACCORDANCE WITH LOOP POLICY.

EACH SCIENTIST PARTICIPATING IN AN LOOP CRUISE HAS A NUMBER OF GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES WITH RESPECT TO CONTRIBUTING TO THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES OF THE CRUISE AS SUMMARIZED BELOW. IN ADDITION , DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC JOB ASSIGNMENTS ARE ADDRESSED IN THE SUBSEQUENT SECTION.

CRUISE OBJECTIVES
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO FAMILIARIZE HIMSELF/HERSELF WITH THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONAL STRATEGIES OF THE EXPEDITION AS OUTLINED IN THE CRUISE PROSPECTUS .

PRECRUISE SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST
EACH PARTICIPANT IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT AN EXPEDITION-SPECIFIC RESEARCH PLAN (SAMPLE AND DATA REQUEST) THAT CONTAINS AN OUTLINE OF THEIR PROPOSED POSTCRUISE RESEARCH. THIS IS TYPICALLY SUBMITTED -3-4 MONTHS BEFORE THE EXPEDITION. THIS REQUEST WILL BE USED TO ALLOCATE CORE SAMPLES AND DATA, AND COORDINATE AND DEFINE ALL PARTICIPANT'S POSTCRUISE RESEARCH.

SPECIAL LAB NEEDS
SCIENTISTS MUST ALERT LOOP AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE IF SPECIAL EQUIPMENT, SUPPLIES, LAB SPACE, ETC ARE NEEDED. ADDITIONAL DETAILS ABOUT SPECIFIC LABS AND SHIPBOARD INSTRUMENTATION CAN BE FOUND AT HTTP://IODP.TAMU.EDU/LABS/
SHIP.HTML .CONTACT THE LOOP EXPEDITION PROJECT MANAGER IF YOU ARE UNSURE
ABOUT LAB FACILITIES OR REQUIREMENTS .

DATA COLLECTION
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS COLLECT, ANALYZE AND COMPILE DATA CONFORMING TO LOOP PROCEDURES AND FORMAT ESTABLISHED FOR EACH LABORATORY STATION AND JOB DESCRIPTION. WORK SHIFTS TYPICALLY ARE 12 HOURS A DAY, 7 DAYS PER WEEK FOR THE DURATION OF THE EXPEDITION. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING AN EXPEDITION SHALL BE INPUT INTO THE SPECIFIED DATABASES AND SHALL BE DOCUMENTED IN THE EXPEDITION REPORT. ALL DATA COLLECTED DURING A CRUISE ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ENTIRE SHIPBOARD PARTY FOR ONE YEAR POSTCRUISE OR FOLLOWING SHORE-BASED SAMPLING. AFTER THIS 1-YEAR MORATORIUM, ALL DATA AND SAMPLES BECOME PUBLIC.

REPORTS
SHIPBOARD SCIENTISTS PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC REPORTS IN THE FORM OF SITE CHAPTERS FOR THE EXPEDITION REPORT (ER) VOLUME AND PRESENT THEIR FINDINGS TO THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN SHIPBOARD SCIENCE MEETINGS .THEY ALSO ASSIST THE CO-CHIEFS IN WRITING SUMMARY REPORTS SUCH AS THE WEEKLY REPORT , SITE SUMMARY REPORT, AND EXPEDITION SUMMARY CHAPTER FOR THE ER. AUTHORSHIP OF CRUISE REPORTS IS COLLECTIVE; INDIVIDUALS CONTRIBUTE ACCORDING TO THEIR JOB ASSIGNMENT AND SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE.

SAMPLING
SAMPLING OF THE CORES ON THE SHIP IS PERFORMED BY ALL MEMBERS OF THE SCIENTIFIC PARTY. SCIENTISTS ARE ROUTINELY ASSIGNED CORE-SAMPLING SHIFTS (NOT MORE THAN 2 HOURS/DAY) DURING THEIR NORMAL WORK SHIFT. WHEN A CORE IS READY FOR SAMPLING, THE ASSIGNED SAMPLING SHIFT TAKES ALL THE SAMPLES DESIGNATED  FOR SHIPBOARD SAMPLING BY THE SAMPLE ALLOCATION COMMITTEE (SAC), UNDER GUIDANCE OF THE SHIPBOARD CURATOR. FOR HIGH RECOVERY CRUISES, PARTICIPANTS ARE ALSO STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO TAKE PART IN POSTCRUISE SAMPLING PARTIES, WHEN SAMPLES CANNOT BE OBTAINED ON BOARD, IF TRAVEL FUNDING IS AVAILABLE.
